batch: Batch114 — Runtime Dependency Closure Board & Build Blocker Evidence
purpose: Make build/runtime blockers visible and auditable before claiming deploy/hosted readiness.
principles: Do not claim npm install, Next build, live HTTP smoke, auth runtime, or hosted demo pass unless the command actually passes.
Source-level validators may pass while runtime/build remains blocked; reports must label this honestly.
node_modules and .next are temporary local artifacts and must not be included in final ZIP artifacts.
No AI SDK, model call, API key, payment, cash marketplace, multi-level referral, fake verified academic data, or public community auto-publish is allowed in this batch.
allowedClaimsWhenOnlySourcePasses: Source-level runtime dependency closure board exists.
The repo can diagnose missing dependency/build/runtime evidence.
Build/runtime/hosted status remains unclaimed unless commands pass.
forbiddenClaimsWhenCommandsFail: Do not say build-ready if next:swc-ready or build:clean fails.
Do not say hosted-ready if hosted URL smoke is skipped or missing.
Do not say auth/session/CSRF runtime is safe if auth runtime smoke fails or is not run.
Do not say production-ready while npm ci/build/live smoke are unresolved.
